  • Flow Channels

Flow Channels#

FlowChannel inherits from PorousLayer so that the channel participates in the same gas-transport pipeline as the GDL and MPL. It holds channel geometry (width, height, length, number of parallel channels) and delegates gas-transport resistance to a pluggable ChannelGasResistanceModel.

Two resistance models are available:

  • ChannelGasResistanceModel — Sherwood-number approach (Kim et al. 2022) combining a diffusion sub-resistance and a convection sub-resistance.

  • BakerChannelGasResistanceModel — empirical correlations from Baker et al. (2009) for the sub-resistances.

Component#

class marapendi.channel.flow_channels.FlowChannel(thickness=0.001, gas=<factory>, temperature=300.0, pressure=100000.0, porosity=1, effective_gas_diffusion_ratio=1, pore_diameter=1000000000000.0, transport_resistance_model=<factory>, two_phase_transport_model=<factory>, non_wetting_saturation=0, thermal_conductivity=1000000000000.0, absolute_permeability=1000000.0, relative_permeability_exponent=3, contact_angle=120.0, non_wetting_phase='water', wetting_phase='gas', breakthrough_pressure=None, reactant='o2', inlet_stoichiometry=0, inlet_gas_flow_rate=1e-12, inlet_liquid_flow_rate=0, inlet_liquid_saturation=0, width=0.001, height=0.001, length=0.1, channel_land_ratio=1.0, n_parallel=14)[source]#

Bases: PorousLayer

Flow channel geometry and state of a single fuel-cell side.

Inherits porous-layer geometry from PorousLayer so that it can be used in the same gas-transport pipeline.

Parameters:

  • thickness (float)

  • gas (GasState)

  • temperature (float)

  • pressure (float)

  • porosity (float)

  • effective_gas_diffusion_ratio (float)

  • pore_diameter (float)

  • transport_resistance_model (ChannelGasResistanceModel)

  • two_phase_transport_model (DarcyTransportModel)

  • non_wetting_saturation (float)

  • thermal_conductivity (float)

  • absolute_permeability (float)

  • relative_permeability_exponent (float)

  • contact_angle (float)

  • non_wetting_phase (str)

  • wetting_phase (str)

  • breakthrough_pressure (float)

  • reactant (str)

  • inlet_stoichiometry (float)

  • inlet_gas_flow_rate (float)

  • inlet_liquid_flow_rate (float)

  • inlet_liquid_saturation (float)

  • width (float)

  • height (float)

  • length (float)

  • channel_land_ratio (float)

  • n_parallel (int)

reactant#

Primary reactant species (‘o2’ or ‘h2’).

Type:

str

inlet_stoichiometry#

Inlet stoichiometric ratio (actual / required flow).

Type:

float

inlet_gas_flow_rate#

Volumetric gas flow rate at the channel inlet (m³/s).

Type:

float

inlet_liquid_flow_rate#

Liquid flow rate at the channel inlet (m³/s).

Type:

float

inlet_liquid_saturation#

Liquid saturation fraction at the inlet.

Type:

float

width, height, length

Channel cross-section width, height, and length (m).

Type:

float

channel_land_ratio#

Ratio of channel width to land width.

Type:

float

n_parallel#

Number of parallel channels.

Type:

int

transport_resistance_model#

Correlation for the channel gas transport resistance.

Type:

ChannelGasResistanceModel

channel_land_ratio: float = 1.0#
height: float = 0.001#
inlet_gas_flow_rate: float = 1e-12#
inlet_liquid_flow_rate: float = 0#
inlet_liquid_saturation: float = 0#
inlet_stoichiometry: float = 0#
length: float = 0.1#
n_parallel: int = 14#
reactant: str = 'o2'#
transport_resistance_model: ChannelGasResistanceModel#
width: float = 0.001#

Transport models#

class marapendi.channel.gas_transport_resistance.ChannelGasResistanceModel(sherwood=4.0, B_ch=1.0)[source]#

Bases: object

Channel gas transport resistance using a Sherwood-number approach.

References

Kim, H. et al. Int. J. Heat Mass Transf. 183, 122106 (2022).

Parameters:

  • sherwood (float)

  • B_ch (float)

B_ch: float = 1.0#
convection_resistance(channel, volume_flow_rate)[source]#

Convection resistance assuming average inlet/outlet concentration (s/m).

gas_transport_resistance(channel, state, species, volume_flow_rate=None)[source]#

Total gas transport resistance for species in channel (s/m).

molecular_diffusion_resistance(channel, diffusion_coefficient)[source]#

Diffusion resistance via Sherwood number (s/m).

sherwood: float = 4.0#
total_resistance(channel, diffusion_coefficient, volume_flow_rate)[source]#

Total channel resistance: diffusion + convection (s/m).

class marapendi.channel.gas_transport_resistance.BakerChannelGasResistanceModel(sherwood=4.0, B_ch=1.0, A_ch=1.0)[source]#

Bases: ChannelGasResistanceModel

Empirical channel gas transport resistance (Baker et al. 2009).

Overrides the diffusion and convection sub-resistances with the empirical correlations from Baker et al.; total_resistance() is inherited from ChannelGasResistanceModel.

Parameters:

  • sherwood (float)

  • B_ch (float)

  • A_ch (float)

A_ch#

Coefficient for the diffusion resistance term.

Type:

float

B_ch#

Coefficient for the convection resistance term (inherited).

Type:

float

References

Baker et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 156, B991 (2009).

A_ch: float = 1.0#
convection_resistance(channel, volume_flow_rate)[source]#

Convection resistance using Baker’s length/half-width formula (s/m).

molecular_diffusion_resistance(channel, diffusion_coefficient)[source]#

Diffusion resistance using Baker’s half-width correlation (s/m).
